grounded_claim: ENPP1 inhibitor with ultralong drug-target residence time; ENPP3-selective; selectivity built at pocket periphery not the metal
note: Published 2025-09-05; preprint 10.1101/2025.05.18.654655. Anchors our 'optimize residence/periphery not the conserved Zn' thesis.

grounded_claim: Oral ENPP1 inhibitor designed using generative AI as next-gen STING modulator for solid tumors; highly ENPP3-selective
note: Insilico Medicine; validates generative-AI design + paralog-selectivity framing as a real competitive arm.

grounded_claim: Crystal structures of human ENPP1 in apo and bound forms — source of 6WEW receptor + native ligand TZV
note: Dennis et al.; primary structural basis of our docking receptor and the native-ligand redocking control.

grounded_claim: ENPP1 is an innate immune checkpoint of the anticancer cGAMP-STING pathway in breast cancer

topic: ENPP3 is a bona fide cGAMP hydrolase (paralog matters)
grounded_claim: ENPP3 identified as a major cGAMP hydrolase and innate immune checkpoint
note: Direct biological justification for counter-screening ENPP3, not just ENPP2.

topic: Extracellular STING route / ENPP1 landscape review
grounded_claim: Stimulating STING for cancer therapy: taking the extracellular route
Ecto-NPP1 inhibitors: research progress and prospects
note: Chemotype/landscape grounding for sulfamide vs hydroxamate vs phosphonate classes.

grounded_claim: Inhibition of ENPP1: a med-chem approach at the crossroad of immunotherapy and metabolic modulation
note: Recent secondary source on ENPP1 inhibitor design.

grounded_claim: Discovery of orally bioavailable phthalazinone analogues as an ENPP1 inhibitor for STING-mediated cancer immunotherapy
Screening and X-ray structure-based optimization of autotaxin (ENPP2) inhibitors
note: ENPP2 pocket differs from ENPP1 — supports ENPP2 divergent-residue (His->Leu, Ser->Phe) argument.

topic: Potent autotaxin inhibitors bind hydrophobic pocket+channel
grounded_claim: Identification of potent autotaxin inhibitors binding both hydrophobic pockets and channels in the catalytic domain
note: ENPP2 has a hydrophobic lipid channel absent in ENPP1 — structural basis for ENPP2 selectivity being 'easy'.

grounded_claim: Cytotoxic evaluation of YSL-109 (hydroxamate) in TNBC and toxicological evaluations
note: Supports (does not prove) hydroxamate warhead tox-liability caveat behind the demotion.
